headline: US Semis See Shifts; Fed Hints at Hikes; AI Earnings Mixed
summary: Apple briefly surpassed Nvidia in market cap amid AI chip shifts. The Dallas Fed President advocated for higher rates, while key earnings from AEHR and ASML showed strong AI-driven guidance. China's CXMT seeks an $8.6B IPO, highlighting domestic chip ambitions.

bullets.lead: Apple briefly became the world's most valuable company.

segments.text: The tech giant temporarily unseated Nvidia, reflecting a shift in AI bets and market valuations.

sources.title: Apple unseats Nvidia to become world's most valuable company as AI bets shift
Apple briefly leapfrogs Nvidia as world’s most valuable company
Apple dethrones Nvidia to regain title of world’s most valuable company
Apple Briefly Unseats Nvidia As World’s Largest Company

bullets.lead: TSMC plans significant US investment and faces capacity constraints.

segments.text: The company added $100 billion to its US spending plan while limiting mature-node expansion and pushing back on ASML equipment pricing.

sources.title: TSMC Adds $100 Billion to Its U.S. Spending Plan
TSMC limits mature-node expansion and eases pressure on PMIC suppliers

segments.text: TSMC's Q2 earnings are anticipated to show a 59% net profit increase, with investors focused on AI demand and CoWoS capacity updates.

bullets.lead: Samsung is expanding its foundry capabilities and considering outsourcing.

segments.text: Samsung Electronics is nearing KRW 10 trillion in equipment orders for its Pyeongtaek Plant 5 (P5) for advanced DRAM and HBM.
The company is also considering outsourcing the back-end design for Google's 2nm TPU I/O die due to surging 2nm foundry demand.

bullets.lead: Global semiconductor equipment sales forecasts have been raised.

segments.text: SEMI projects $165.9 billion in sales by 2026, driven by strong AI-related demand for leading-edge technologies.

bullets.lead: Dallas Fed President Logan called for 'modestly' higher interest rates.

segments.text: This stance comes as cooler inflation may not be sufficient to prevent further Fed rate increases.

sources.title: Cooler Inflation May Not Be Enough to Stave Off Fed Rate Increases
Dallas Fed President Logan calls for 'modestly' higher interest rates

bullets.lead: The dollar saw slight weakness as tech sell-off concerns eased.

segments.text: This led to a 'strong-start, weak-finish' pattern in the stock market.

sources.title: [뉴욕환시] 달러 약보합…증시 기술주 투매 진정에 '전강후약'

bullets.lead: Aehr Test Systems reported strong Q4 earnings and robust FY2027 guidance.

segments.text: The company projects $130-$150 million in revenue, driven by accelerating demand for wafer-level burn-in from AI processes.

bullets.lead: ASML exceeded expectations with strong quarterly results and raised its full-year net sales guidance.

segments.text: The company now expects €43-45 billion, fueled by strong demand for semiconductor manufacturing equipment.

bullets.lead: Netflix reported slowing revenue growth for the second quarter.

segments.text: The streaming giant's revenue increased 13% to $12.6 billion, but growth has decelerated.

sources.title: Netflix Revenue Grows 13% to $12.6 Billion
Netflix Reports Slowing Revenue Growth for Second Quarter

bullets.lead: China's CXMT is seeking an $8.6 billion IPO in Shanghai.

segments.text: This move highlights China's ambition to bolster its domestic memory chip industry.

sources.title: China Memory Chipmaker CXMT Seeks $8.6 Billion in Shanghai IPO

bullets.lead: DeepSeek is preparing for an IPO on Shanghai’s STAR Market.

segments.text: The company is raising $7.4 billion at a $71 billion pre-money valuation with nearly $500 million ARR.

bullets.lead: China's Moonshot AI claims its Kimi K3 model can rival OpenAI and Anthropic.

segments.text: This indicates increasing competition in the global AI landscape from Chinese developers.

sources.title: China's Moonshot AI claims Kimi K3 can rival OpenAI and Anthropic

bullets.lead: Meta and Anthropic are in talks for a potential $10 billion compute lease deal.

segments.text: This significant agreement would involve Meta selling computing power to Anthropic.

sources.title: Meta, Anthropic in talks for potential $10 billion compute lease deal, source says
Meta, Anthropic in talks for potential $10 billion compute lease deal, source says
Meta and Anthropic in talks for up to $10bn data centre deal
Meta in Talks to Sell Computing Power to Anthropic, NYT Reports

bullets.lead: Elon Musk announced Grok Build is now open source.

segments.text: This contributes to the growing landscape of US-based open-source large language models.

bullets.lead: Anthropic is reportedly planning investor meetings for an IPO as early as October.

segments.text: This follows a $65 billion financing round last May.

watchlist.note: Watch for further hawkish commentary after Dallas Fed President Logan's call for higher rates.

x_themes.topic: AEHR's Strong Earnings and AI/Optical/Memory Testing Outlook
x_themes.summary: Aehr Test Systems ($AEHR) reported strong Q4 earnings and an impressive FY2027 revenue guidance of $130-$150M, driven by accelerating demand for wafer-level burn-in from AI processors, silicon photonics, and new opportunities in HBM/NAND memory testing.

x_themes.topic: ASML Strong Earnings, Raised Guidance, and Capacity Expansion
x_themes.summary: ASML reported stronger-than-expected quarterly results and significantly boosted its full-year net sales guidance to €43-45 billion, driven by strong demand for semiconductor manufacturing equipment, and plans to increase both low NA EUV and DUV immersion capacity by 30% for 2027 and potentially 2028.

x_themes.topic: TSMC Q2 Earnings Expectations and AI Outlook
x_themes.summary: TSMC's Q2 earnings are expected to show a 59% net profit increase and record gross/operating margins, with investors keenly awaiting updates on AI demand health, CoWoS capacity, N2/N3 ramp, and potential price increases, alongside a raised 2024-2029 AI semiconductor revenue CAGR forecast of 70-80%.

Samsung Electronics is nearing over KRW 10 trillion in semiconductor equipment orders for its Pyeongtaek Plant 5 (P5), which will produce cutting-edge DRAM and HBM, with Phase 1 potentially operational by next year to meet surging memory demand.

x_themes.topic: Memory Market Dynamics: DRAM Price Pushback & Strong NAND Demand
x_themes.summary: Customers are pushing back against 30% DRAM price increases, leading to a lowered Q3 DRAM price growth forecast, while NAND demand is strong due to KV cache offloading and substitution for expensive DRAM.

x_themes.topic: Samsung Considers Outsourcing Google 2nm TPU I/O Die Design
x_themes.summary: Samsung Electronics is considering outsourcing the back-end design for Google's 2nm TPU I/O die due to a staff shortage from surging 2nm foundry demand, with Google's compute processor to be made by TSMC at 1.4nm and the I/O die by Samsung at 2nm.

x_themes.topic: SEMI Raises Global Semiconductor Equipment Sales Forecasts
x_themes.summary: SEMI has raised its global semiconductor equipment sales forecasts for the next three years, projecting $165.9 billion in 2026, driven by strong AI-related demand for leading-edge logic, memory chips, and advanced packaging.

x_themes.topic: PJM Capacity Auction Reveals Structural Power Shortfall
x_themes.summary: The 2028/2029 PJM capacity auction confirmed a persistent structural shortfall in dispatchable and reliably accredited capacity, with the clearing price hitting an administratively imposed ceiling of $325/MW-day despite an estimated unconstrained price of $554.72/MW-day, highlighting long-term scarcity.

DeepSeek is raising $7.4 billion at a $71 billion pre-money valuation with nearly $500 million ARR, and is preparing for an IPO on Shanghai’s STAR Market as early as next year, while also seeking US dollar-denominated capital from overseas investors.
Anthropic is reportedly planning investor meetings in the coming weeks for an IPO as early as October, following a $65 billion financing round at a $965 billion valuation last May.

Nvidia has introduced new Thor robotics computer modules, the T3000 and T2000, which are half the size and power of previous versions while delivering similar AI inference performance, with shipments scheduled for Q1 2027.

x_themes.topic: Apple's Potential AI Chip Acquisitions and Capex Increase
x_themes.summary: Apple is reportedly looking to acquire AI chip companies to bolster its own AI infrastructure, potentially leading to a significant increase in its AI capex spend, which could be bullish for related sectors like optical networking and foundries.
Elon Musk announced that Grok Build is now open source, with Grok 4.5 continuously improving, contributing to the growing landscape of US-based open-source large language models.

x_themes.topic: TSMC Pushing Back on ASML Equipment Pricing
x_themes.summary: TSMC is reportedly pushing back against ASML's plans to raise equipment prices, although ASML intends to proceed with the price increases.
